output:
[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: apply_threshold_monitoring; apply_breach_projection
Suppress: limit_blindness; gradual_overload_normalization
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

This is a stepwise simulation with threshold-dependent recharge. Let me trace it year by year.

**Setup:** Start = 1,000,000 m³. Each year: add recharge (based on current level), then subtract 100,000 m³.

**Phase 1 — Level > 600,000:** Recharge = 60,000, net loss = 40,000/year

| Year | End Level |
|------|-----------|
| 0 | 1,000,000 |
| 1 | 960,000 |
| 2 | 920,000 |
| ... | ... |
| 10 | 600,000 |

After **10 years**, the aquifer hits 600,000.

**Phase 2 — 400,000 ≤ Level ≤ 600,000:** Recharge = 40,000, net loss = 60,000/year

| Year | End Level |
|------|-----------|
| 11 | 540,000 |
| 12 | 480,000 |
| 13 | 420,000 |
| 14 | 360,000 |

After year 13, level is 420,000 (still ≥ 400,000, so recharge stays 40,000). After year 14 it drops to 360,000.

**Phase 3 — Level < 400,000:** Recharge = 20,000, net loss = 80,000/year

| Year | End Level |
|------|-----------|
| 15 | 280,000 |
| 16 | **200,000** |

**The aquifer first reaches 200,000 m³ in Year 16.**
